qas.q: Has a Container Control Officer (CCO) been appointed in writing?
Does the CCO or personnel who are charged with carrying out container management functions possess the mandatory training certification?
qas.a: A copy of the training certification and/or orders designating the individual as a CSC certification/recertification inspector will be retained by the Service or Agency CCO's Unit/HQ

qas.q: Is the CCO providing the ISO Container reports in the DoD system of record Army Container Asset Management System (ACAMS) and the Marine Corps Accountable Property System of Record (APSR) Global Combat Support System - Marine Corps?
qas.a: The DoD Components will maintain repositories for CSC inspection records for their Service or agency owned ISO Containers, and ISO-configured tactical shelter and equipment in ACAMS, the inspector will include : ISO number of the equipment, date of last inspection, identification of the inspector, and state "CSC Recertification, No Repairs Required."

qas.q: Are containers examined for serviceability IAW MIL-HDBK-138B or IICL by certified school trained and appointed inspectors every 30 months from the 5th year from date of manufactureer, or after any major repairs and the status reported in ACAMS?
qas.a: Containers will be examined by certified school-trained and appointed inspectors every 30 months from the fifth year from the date, and will report any major repairs and status via ACAMS.

qas.q: Is the CCO or Trusted Assistant applying new DD Form 2282 on all inspeected containers?
qas.a: The inspector will punch the month of expiration on the DD Form 2282 (30 months from the date of inspection)

qas.q: Convention for Safe Container (CSC) Reinspection Decal
qas.a: Shows what year it will expire depending on the color of the CSC.
